title: SSC CGL 2026 Sectional Timing Tier I 19 19

questions.question: Find the amount received on ₹4250 at 4% rate of simple interest per annum after 7 years.

In a line of tags fixed on crates, Tag X is 12th from the left end. Tag Y is 18th from the right end and is to the right of X. X is shifted 4 places to the left. After this shift, the number of tags between X and Y becomes 9. How many tags are there in the line?
questions.explanation: Let N = total tags After shifting, X's new position from the left = 12 − 4 = 8 Position of Y from the left = N − 18 + 1 = N − 17 Tags between X and Y = (N − 17) − 8 − 1 = N − 26 N − 26 = 9 N = 35

questions.question: In a certain code language, 'solar power grid' is written as '4n 7p 2k' and 'grid is stable' is written as '6m 2k 9t'. How will 'grid' be written in that language?
questions.explanation: 'solar power grid' → 4n 7p 2k 'grid is stable' → 6m 2k 9t Common word is 'grid' Common code is 2k So, 'grid' is written as 2k

questions.question: Salman is the father-in-law of Javed. Atif is the brother of Ayesha. Salim is the father of Salman. Ayesha is the wife of Javed. Heena is the mother of Atif. Salim is the husband of Sheenam. If Ayesha is related to Javed in the same way Heena is related to _________.
questions.explanation: Salman is the father-in-law of Javed. Atif is the brother of Ayesha. Salim is the father of Salman. Ayesha is the wife of Javed. Heena is the mother of Atif. Salim is the husband of Sheenam. ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p30405/image035.png) Ayesha is the wife of Javed. In the same way, Heena is the wife of Salman.

questions.question: Read the following statement carefully and identify the assumption(s) that is/are implicit. Statement: The metro authority plans to add a small surcharge on tickets bought during peak hours to spread passenger demand across the day. Assumptions: I. Some commuters will change their travel time or choice if peak-hour travel becomes slightly costlier. II. The metro authority can reliably identify peak-hour purchases and collect the surcharge.
options: Both Assumption I and Assumption II are implicit
Neither Assumption I nor Assumption II is implicit
questions.explanation: Statement: The metro authority plans to add a small surcharge on tickets bought during peak hours to spread passenger demand across the day. This indicates that the authority believes pricing can influence commuter behavior and that the system can implement the surcharge effectively. Assumption I: The purpose of adding a surcharge is to influence demand. If commuters did not change their behavior in response to the price increase, the surcharge would not help spread demand. Therefore, this assumption is implicit. Assumption II: For the surcharge policy to work, the metro authority must be able to determine which tickets are bought during peak hours and collect the additional charge. Without this capability, the plan cannot be implemented effectively. Hence, this assumption is also implicit. So, both Assumption I and Assumption II are implicit.

options: Flat No. 11-D, Block C, Seaside Hights, Plot No. 4/2, Jayadev Vihar, Bhubaneswar, Odisha - 751013
Flat No. 11-D, Block C, Seaside Heights, Plot No. 4-2, Jayadev Vihar, Bhubaneswar, Odisha - 751013

questions.question: Study the given pattern carefully and select the number from among the given options that can replace the question mark (?). 5 • 8 = 17 13 • 3 = 166 29 • 5 = ?
questions.explanation: Here, the pattern is: 5 • 8 = 5 x 5 - 8 = 17 And 13 • 3 = 13 x 13 - 3 = 166 Therefore, 29 • 5 = 29 x 29 - 5 = 836

questions.question: Identify the fruit from the given option that belong to the following class: Bell, Speaking, Alarm clock
questions.explanation: Bell, Speaking and alarm clock make sounds. Similarly, explosion makes sound.

questions.question: Pick the odd one: 3 - 28, 20 - 29, 13 - 34, 35 - 51, 11 - 47
questions.explanation: Here, the pattern is: 3 - 28: Here, 28 - 3 = 25 and 25 is a perfect square number. 20 - 29: Here, 29 - 20 = 9 and 9 is a perfect square number. 13 - 34: Here, 34 - 13 = 21 and 21 is not a perfect square number. 35 - 51: Here, 51 - 35 = 16 and 16 is a perfect square number. 11 - 47: Here, 47 - 11 = 36 and 36 is a perfect square number.

questions.question: How many meaningful four-letter English words can be formed using the First, Second, Fourth and Fifth letters of the word 'CHAIRMAN' (when counted from left to right) using each letter only once in each word?
questions.explanation: CHAIRMAN 12345678 Select the First, Second, Fourth and Fifth letters of the word 'CHAIRMAN' - CHIR 1245 The word formed is - RICH Thus, only one word is formed. Meaning of RICH: 1) having a lot of money or property 2) containing a lot of something

questions.question: If M ÷ N means M is the sister of N, M + N means M is the husband of N, and M X N means M is the son of N, which of the following shows that S is the son-in-law of V?
questions.explanation: Option (a): V ÷ C + K ÷ S V ÷ C means V is the sister of C C + K means C is the husband of K K ÷ S means K is the sister of S ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p26746/image008.png) S is the brother-in-law or sister-in-law of V's brother. So, option (a) is incorrect. Option (b): S + O ÷ B X V S + O means S is the husband of O O ÷ B means O is the sister of B B X V means B is the son of V ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p26746/image009.png) S is the son-in-law of V. So, option (b) is correct. Option (c): P ÷ S + T ÷ V P ÷ S means P is the sister of S S + T means S is the husband of T T ÷ V means T is the sister of V ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p26746/image010.png) S is the brother-in-law of V. So, option (c) is incorrect. Option (d): B X V + E ÷ S B X V means B is the son of V V + E means V is the husband of E E ÷ S means E is the sister of S ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p26746/image011.png) S is the brother-in-law or sister-in-law of V. So, option (d) is incorrect.

questions.question: Select the option which is related to the third term in the same way as the second term is related to the first term and the sixth term is related to the fifth term. ADVICE: BCUJBF :: BRIGHT: ? :: CANDLE: BBMCKF
questions.explanation: All the vowels are interchanged with the immediately next alphabet and all the consonants are interchanged with the immediately previous alphabet. ADVICE ----- BCUJBF CANDLE ----- BBMCKF Similarly, BRIGHT ----- AQJFGS So, option (d) is the correct answer.

questions.question: The position of how many letters will remain unchanged if all the consonants followed by vowels in the word 'SWEATER' are arranged in English alphabetical order?
questions.explanation: SWEATER After re-arrangement: RSTWAEE So, the position of 'E' remains unchanged. Thus, option (d) is the correct answer.

questions.question: Select the option which is related to the third number in the same way as the second number is related to the first number and the sixth number is related to the fifth number. 45: 86 :: 38: ? :: 63 : 122
questions.explanation: 45: 45 x 2 - 4 = 90 - 4 = 86 63: 63 x 2 - 4 = 126 - 4 = 122 Similarly, 38: 38 x 2 - 4 = 76 - 4 = 72 So, option (a) is the correct answer.

questions.question: Select the number from among the given options that can replace the question mark (?) in the following series. 91, 181, 360, 717, ?
In a certain code language, BELOVED is coded as 65, and FLOWER is coded as 79.How will QUIVER be coded in that language?
questions.explanation: Here, the positional values of the letters in English alphabetical order are considered. ABCDEFGHIJKLM 12345678910111213 NOPQRSTUVWXYZ 14151617181920212223242526 BELOVED - 2 + 5 + 12 + 15 + 22 + 5 + 4 = 65. FLOWER - 6 + 12 + 15 + 23 + 5 + 18 = 79. Similarly, QUIVER - 17 + 21 + 9 + 22 + 5 + 18 = 92.

questions.question: Consider the following statements about Gross National Product (GNP): a. GNP includes the value of all goods and services produced by a country's residents, regardless of whether production occurs domestically or abroad. b. GNP excludes the income earned by foreigners within the country. c. GNP is always greater than GDP.
options: Only a and b are correct
Only b and c are correct
Only a and c are correct
questions.explanation: • GNP includes the value of goods and services produced by a country's residents regardless of location • It excludes income earned by foreigners within the country. • GNP is not always greater than GDP, as it depends on net factor income from abroad, which could be positive or negative. Further Insight: • GDP at factor cost = GDP at market prices - indirect taxes + subsidies • GNI=GDP+Net Factor Income from Abroad • Gross National Income (GNI)at market prices = GDP at market prices + taxes less subsidies on production and imports (Net receivable from abroad) + Compensation of Employees (Net receivable from abroad) + property income (Net receivable from abroad) • Net National Income (NNI) at market prices = GNI at market prices - consumption of fixed capital • Net National Disposable Income(NNDI) = NNI + net taxes on income and wealth receivable from abroad + net social contributions and benefits receivable from abroad.

questions.question: Under which article, remedies for enforcement of rights conferred by Part III are provided?
questions.explanation: Article 32 of the Indian Constitution, titled "Right to Constitutional Remedies," provides the mechanism for the enforcement of Fundamental Rights, which are listed in Part III of the Constitution. This article empowers every citizen to move the Supreme Court of India directly if their Fundamental Rights are violated. Because it makes the other rights effective, Dr. B.R. Ambedkar called Article 32 the "heart and soul of the Constitution." Explanation of Other Options: Article 21: This is a fundamental right itself ("Protection of life and personal liberty"), not the remedy for its violation. Article 226: This article empowers the High Courts to issue writs for the enforcement of Fundamental Rights and for "any other purpose." While it is a crucial remedy, Article 32 is the fundamental right within Part III that guarantees the remedy. Article 14: This is the fundamental right to "equality before law." Further insights: Under Article 32, the Supreme Court can issue five types of writs: Habeas Corpus, Mandamus, Prohibition, Certiorari, and Quo Warranto. The right to move the Supreme Court under Article 32 cannot be suspended except as otherwise provided for by the Constitution (e.g., during a national emergency).

questions.question: Consider the correct statement about the Prahdhan Mantri Shram Yogi Maan Dhan Yojana. A. Age group should be of 18-40 years B. After attaining the age of 60 yrs, beneficiaries are entitled to receive minimum monthly assured pension of Rs.5000/-. C. On death of the beneficiary, spouse is eligible for 50% monthly pension.
options: Only B and C are correct
A, B and C are Correct
Only A and C are correct
questions.explanation: A. Age group should be of 18-40 years This statement is CORRECT. The Pradhan Mantri Shram Yogi Maan-Dhan (PM-SYM) is a voluntary and contributory pension scheme for unorganized sector workers. The entry age for subscribing to the scheme is between 18 and 40 years. B. After attaining the age of 60 yrs, beneficiaries are entitled to receive minimum monthly assured pension of Rs.5000/-. This statement is INCORRECT. While beneficiaries are entitled to a pension after attaining the age of 60, the minimum assured monthly pension amount is ₹3,000, not ₹5,000. C. On death of the beneficiary, spouse is eligible for 50% monthly pension. This statement is CORRECT. This refers to the family pension benefit. If the beneficiary dies after the pension has started (i.e., after age 60), their spouse is entitled to receive 50% of the beneficiary's pension as a family pension for the rest of their life.

questions.question: Consider the following statements regarding the first Field Training Exercise (FTX) conducted under the Indo-Pacific Logistics Network (IPLN): i) The exercise was hosted by India at the Andaman and Nicobar Islands to demonstrate maritime dominance. ii) The primary objective of the FTX was to enhance disaster response capabilities and civilian assistance during large-scale natural disasters. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanation: Statement (ii) is Correct The first FTX under the Indo-Pacific Logistics Network was aimed at improving coordination for Humanitarian Assistance and Disaster Relief (HADR) operations. Statement (i) is Incorrect Statement (i) says the exercise was hosted to demonstrate "maritime dominance." This is incorrect because: The focus of IPLN is logistics coordination and disaster response, not power projection. The objective was humanitarian cooperation, not military signalling. The phrase "demonstrate maritime dominance" makes the statement misleading. Further Insights IPLN focuses on logistics sharing among Indo-Pacific partner nations. HADR stands for Humanitarian Assistance and Disaster Relief. The Indo-Pacific region is highly vulnerable to cyclones, tsunamis, and earthquakes. Logistics cooperation reduces response time during natural disasters. Such exercises strengthen regional strategic trust without being direct military alliances.

questions.question: According to the Koppen's scheme of climatic classification, which among the following statements is/are correct? I. North-Western Gujarat is part of Semi-arid steppe climate. II. Most of peninsular India south of Tropic of cancer has tropical savannah climate. III. Any region of India doesn't have a polar type climate. Select the answer using the codes below.
questions.explanation: ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/other/er241205imgst2.png) Koppen's scheme of climatic classification uses letter symbols to denote climatic types. Each type is further sub-divided into sub-types on the basis of seasonal variations in the distributional pattern of rainfall and temperature. It uses S for semi-arid and W for arid and the following small letters to define sub-types: f (sufficient precipitation), m (rain forest despite a dry monsoon season), w (dry season in winter), h (dry and hot), c (less than four months with mean temperature over 10°C), and g (Gangetic plain). Based on this classification Indian climate can be divided into following types:Type of ClimateAreas Amw Monsoon with short dry seasonWest coast of India south of Go. As - Monsoon with dry summerCoromandel coast of Tamil Nadu Aw - Tropical savannahMost of the Peninsular plateaus, south of the Tropic of Cancer. (Statement II is correct) Bwhw - Semi-arid steppe climateNorth-western Gujarat, some parts of western Rajasthan and Punjab. (Statement I is correct) Bwhw - Hot desertExtreme western Rajasthan Cwg - Monsoon with dry winterGanga plain, eastern Rajasthan, northern Madhya Pradesh, most of North-east India Dfc - Cold humid winter with short summerArunachal Pradesh E - Polar typeJammu and Kashmir, Himachal Pradesh and Uttarakhand. (Statement III is incorrect) Source: NCERT

questions.question: Which of the following is a dance form from Kerala that involves performers wearing masks and depicting characters from the Ramayana?
questions.explanation: • Kathakali is a classical dance form of Kerala where performers wear elaborate masks and costumes to depict characters from epics like the Ramayana. • Mohiniyattam: A graceful dance performed by women, focused on storytelling but without masks. • Theyyam: A ritualistic dance performed by men in the northern parts of Kerala, often connected with local deities. • Thullal: A solo performance with satirical commentary on mythological stories. Further Insight: • Dances of Kerala • Kathakali - Classical dance-drama from Kerala, known for its elaborate costumes and makeup, frequently asked in UPSC/SSC exams. • Mohiniyattam - Classical dance form performed by women, depicting feminine love and devotion. • Thiruvathirakali - Performed by women during the festival of Onam. • Kummatti - Mask dance performed during Onam celebrations. • Theyyam

questions.question: The Gandak river rises from:
questions.explanation: The Gandak River, also known as the Narayani in southern Nepal, originates from the Nhubine Himal Glacier in the Mustang region of Nepal. Its source is located near the border of Tibet, but it is firmly within Nepalese territory. The river then flows south through Nepal before entering India in the state of Bihar. Explanation of Other Options: Bhutan: Rivers like the Manas and Sankosh originate in or flow through Bhutan. India: While the Gandak flows through India, it does not originate there. Tibet: The source of the Gandak is near the Tibetan border, but the actual glacier is in Nepal. Major rivers like the Brahmaputra (Yarlung Tsangpo) and Indus rise in Tibet. Further insights: The Gandak is a major left-bank tributary of the Ganges river in India. The river's basin is known for its rich biodiversity and is home to the Chitwan National Park in Nepal and the Valmiki National Park in India. It is one of the major river systems of Nepal.

questions.question: Which of the following statement(s) is/are correct regarding the "Phylum Mollusca"? A. These animals have a soft, unsegmented body, with a hard, calcareous shell to protect the soft body. B. They have muscular feet to help in locomotion and also to act as a weapon in some cases.
questions.explanation: Phylum Mollusca - These animals have a soft, unsegmented body, with a hard, calcareous shell to protect the soft body. They have muscular feet to help in locomotion and also to act as a weapon in some cases. Examples: snails, slugs, oysters, mussels, clams, squids, and octopuses. Main Characters: - - Unsegmented soft-bodied animals terrestrial or aquatic, - Exoskeleton in the form of a shell. When present shell is usually univalved or bivalved; internal shell present in some. - Sexes separate or united. - Have a muscular foot for locomotion.

questions.question: Match the following traditional theatre from with their respective state: Column 1 Column 2 i. Maach a. Tamil Nadu ii. Bhaona b. Konkan and Goa regions iii. Dashavatar c. Assam iv. Therukoothu d. Madhya Pradesh
questions.explanation: Maach is the traditional theatre form of Madhya Pradesh. The term Maach is used for the stage itself as also for the play. In this theatre form songs are given prominence in between the dialogues. The term for dialogue in this form is bol and rhyme in narration is termed vanag. The tunes of this theatre form are known as rangat. Bhaona is a presentation of the Ankia Naat of Assam. In Bhaona cultural glimpses of Assam, Bengal Orissa, Mathura and Brindavan can be seen. The Sutradhaar, or narrator begins the story, first in Sanskrit and then in either Brajboli or Assamese. Dashavatar is the most developed theatre form of the Konkan and Goa regions. The performers personify the ten incarnations of Lord Vishnu-the god of preservation and creativity. The ten incarnations are Matsya (fish), Kurma (tortoise), Varaha (boar), Narsimha (lion-man), Vaman (dwarf), Parashuram, Rama, Krishna (or Balram), Buddha and Kalki. Apart from stylized make-up, the Dashavatar performers wear masks of wood and papier mache. Therukoothu, the most popular form of folk drama of Tamil Nadu, literally means "street play". It is mostly performed at the time of annual temple festivals of Mariamman (Rain goddess) to achieve rich harvest. At the core of the extensive repertoire of Therukoothu there is a cycle of eight plays based on the life of Draupadi. Kattiakaran, the Sutradhara of the Therukoothu performance, gives the gist of the play to the audience and Komali entertains the audience with his buffoonery.

questions.question: Which of the following is true about the host of the FIFA Women's World Cup 2027?
options: It will be held in a single city, Rio de Janeiro, at the Maracana Stadium.
It will be hosted across multiple cities in Brazil, including Brasilia and Sao Paulo.
It will take place in Argentina, with Buenos Aires as the primary host city.
It will be hosted in South Africa across five cities.
questions.explanation: Brazil was officially selected to host the 2027 FIFA Women's World Cup by FIFA in May 2024. This marks the first time the tournament will be held in South America. The tournament will be played in eight cities across Brazil. These cities include Rio de Janeiro, Braslia, So Paulo, Belo Horizonte, Fortaleza, Porto Alegre, Recife, and Salvador.

questions.question: Assertion (A): Mahatma Gandhi abruptly called off the Non-Cooperation Movement in February 1922. Reason (R): The incident at Chauri Chaura, where a violent mob set fire to a police station, went against his creed of non-violence (Ahimsa).
options: Both A and R are true, and R explains A.
Both A and R are true, but R does not explain A.
A is true, but R is false.
A is false, but R is true.
questions.explanation: Both Assertion (A) and Reason (R) are true, and R is the correct explanation of A. Assertion (A) is true: Mahatma Gandhi did indeed suspend the Non-Cooperation Movement, which had been gaining momentum across the country, in February 1922. Reason (R) is true: The incident at Chauri Chaura in Uttar Pradesh on February 4, 1922, involved a clash between protestors and police, which resulted in the protestors burning down a police station, killing 22 policemen. Relationship: The violence at Chauri Chaura was the direct trigger for Gandhi's decision. It directly violated the fundamental principle of non-violence (Ahimsa), which was the cornerstone of the entire movement. He felt that the people were not yet ready for a disciplined, non-violent struggle and called it off. Therefore, R correctly explains A. Further insights: The Non-Cooperation Movement was launched in 1920 in response to the Jallianwala Bagh massacre and the Khilafat issue. Gandhi's decision to withdraw the movement was met with disappointment by many nationalist leaders, including Jawaharlal Nehru and Subhas Chandra Bose, who felt it was a mistake to halt the movement when it was at its peak.

questions.question: Consider the following statements with respect Directive Principles of State Policy: I. They contain goals and objectives that society should adopt. II. They enshrine rights outside of Part III of the constitution. Which of the above statements is / are correct?
questions.explanation: The chapter on Directive Principles lists mainly three things: 1) The goals and objectives that we as a society should adopt; 2) Certain rights that individuals should enjoy apart from the Fundamental Rights; and 3) Certain policies that the government should adopt. Source: NCERT.

questions.question: What is the effective resistance between two points A and B, if three resistors of 1Ω each connected in parallel between these two points?
questions.explanation: • If three resistors of 1Ω each are connected in parallel between two points • ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p26475/image002.png) • Effective resistance in parallel ○ 1/Reff. = 1/R1 + 1/R2 + 1/R3 ○ 1/Reff. = 1/1 + 1/1 + 1/1 ○ 1/Reff = 3 ○ Reff = 1/3 Ω Further Insight: PropertySeries CombinationParallel Combination FormulaEffective resistance =R1+R2+R3+..Effective resistance 1/Reff. = 1/R1 + 1/R2 + 1/R3 ​ Total ResistanceIncreases with added resistors.Decreases with added resistors. Current (I)Same through all resistors.Divided among resistors. Voltage (V)Divided across resistors.Same across all resistors. Equivalent ResistanceGreater than the largest individual resistance.Less than the smallest individual resistance. ApplicationsUsed in voltage dividers.Used in power distribution circuits.

questions.question: Which among the following is NOT a local name of Shifting cultivation?
questions.explanation: Shifting Cultivation: It is also called slash and burn agriculture, in which vegetation is usually cleared by fire, and the ashes add to the fertility of the soil. The cultivated patches are very small, and cultivation is done with very primitive tools such as sticks and hoes. After 3 to 5 years when the soil loses its fertility, the farmer shifts to another parts and clears other patch of the forest for cultivation. It is prevalent in tropical region in different names, e.g. Jhuming in North eastern states of India, Milpa in central America and Mexico and Ladang in Indonesia and Malaysia. In different regions of India, it is called in different names - Bewar or Dahiya in Madhya Pradesh, Podu or Penda in Andhra Pradesh, Pama Dabi or Koman in Odisha, Valre or Waltre in Rajasthan, Kuruwa in Jharkhand, Khil in Himalayan region and Kumari in Western Ghats. Neeravari means irrigation in kannada. Source: Human Geography and India People and Economy NCERT

questions.question: The Sabka Bima Sabki Raksha (Amendment of Insurance Laws) Bill, 2025 has been passed by Parliament on 17.12.2025. The bill amended which of the following acts? i. The Insurance Act,1938 ii. The Life Insurance Corporation Act, 1956 iii. The Insurance Regulatory and Development Authority Act, 1999.
questions.explanation: The Sabka Bima Sabki Raksha (Amendment of Insurance Laws) Bill, 2025 has been passed by Parliament on 17.12.2025. The bill amends three Acts related to Insurance sector, namely, The Insurance Act,1938, The Life Insurance Corporation Act, 1956 and The Insurance Regulatory and Development Authority Act, 1999. One of the key features of the bill is to allow up to 100% Foreign Direct Investment in Insurance Companies, opening doors to more foreign players to India. This will help in capital augmentation, adoption of advanced technology and bringing global best practices along with increasing employment opportunities. Increased competition would drive efficiency in products and services proving beneficial for the citizens. Further Insights FDI means investment by a foreign company in an Indian business. The insurance sector is regulated by IRDAI (Insurance Regulatory and Development Authority of India). Higher FDI is expected to improve insurance penetration in India. India's insurance penetration is still lower than many developed countries. The move aims to bring more global expertise and capital into the sector.

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a tool of Fiscal Policy? a. Repo Rate b. Taxation c. Public Expenditure
questions.explanation: The tools of fiscal policy are taxes, expenditure, public debt and a nation's budget. They consist of changes in government revenues or rates of the tax structure so as to encourage or restrict private expenditures on consumption and investment. Repo Rate is a monetary policy tool used by RBI.

questions.question: Which dynasty was the successor of the Tughlaq Dynasty?
questions.explanation: Sayyid dynasty was the successor of the Tughlaq Dynasty. Sayyid Dynasty - It was founded by Khizr khan in 1414 A. D. The reign ended in 1451 A. D. Tughlaq Dynasty - Ghyasuddin Tughlaq, who was the Governor of Punjab during the reign of Ala-ud-din Khilji, ascended the throne in 1320 A. D. and founded the Tughlaq dynasty.

questions.question: The marathon is a long-distance race that is part of the athletics program at the Olympic Games. What is the official, standardized distance of a marathon?
questions.explanation: The official, standardized distance of a marathon is 42.195 kilometers. This is equivalent to 26 miles and 385 yards. This distance has been the international standard for the marathon since 1921. Further insights: The distance of the marathon is based on the length of the race at the 1908 London Olympics. The race started at Windsor Castle and finished in front of the royal box at the White City Stadium, resulting in the unique distance of 26 miles and 385 yards. The event is named after the legend of the Greek messenger Pheidippides, who is said to have run from the battlefield of Marathon to Athens to announce the Greek victory over Persia.

questions.question: Which scientist is incorrectly matched with his contribution to cell theory?
options: Rudolf Virchow - Proposed that cells arise from pre-existing cells.
questions.explanation: ○ The nucleus was discovered by Robert Brown, not Theodor Schwann. Schwann, along with Schleiden, proposed that all animals and plants are made of cells. ○ Robert Hooke coined the term "cell,". ○ Leeuwenhoek observed living cells. ○ Virchow proposed "Omnis cellula e cellula." Further Insight: • Cell Theory • The Cell Theory was developed by three scientists and provides the foundation for modern biology. ○ Matthias Schleiden (1838): Studied plants and concluded that all plant tissues are made of cells. ○ Theodor Schwann (1839): Extended Schleiden's theory to animals, concluding that all animals are also made of cells. ○ Rudolf Virchow (1855): Expanded the theory by introducing the idea that cells arise from pre-existing cells (Omnis cellula e cellula).

questions.question: The principles of India's SAGAR (Security and Growth for All in the Region) initiative are best reflected in which of the following actions?
options: The development of the India-Myanmar-Thailand Trilateral Highway.
Leading the establishment of the International Solar Alliance (ISA).
Providing humanitarian assistance and disaster relief (HADR) to island nations in the Indian Ocean.
Signing a Free Trade Agreement (FTA) with the United Kingdom.
questions.explanation: The action that best reflects the principles of India's SAGAR initiative is providing humanitarian assistance and disaster relief (HADR) to island nations in the Indian Ocean. The SAGAR initiative is India's maritime doctrine and vision for the Indian Ocean Region (IOR). A core tenet of this policy is for India to act as a "first responder" and "net security provider" in the region. Providing HADR to neighboring countries during crises like natural disasters or pandemics (e.g., Mission Sagar) directly embodies the goal of ensuring "Security" (human security) and enabling "Growth" (by helping partners recover and stabilize) for all nations in the region. Explanation of Other Options: The development of the India-Myanmar-Thailand Trilateral Highway: This is a land-based connectivity project under India's 'Act East' policy. While it promotes regional growth, it is not a maritime initiative, which is the specific focus of SAGAR. Leading the establishment of the International Solar Alliance (ISA): This is a major global initiative by India focused on climate change and sustainable energy. It is not specifically limited to the maritime domain or the Indian Ocean Region. Signing a Free Trade Agreement (FTA) with the United Kingdom: This is a bilateral economic agreement. While it promotes growth, it is not a regional initiative focused on the Indian Ocean, which is the geographical context of SAGAR. Further insights: SAGAR (Security and Growth for All in the Region) was a term coined by Prime Minister Narendra Modi in 2015. The initiative aims to deepen economic and security cooperation with maritime neighbors and build their maritime security capabilities. It is a key framework for managing the maritime space of the Indian Ocean and countering influences that might destabilize the region.

questions.question: Identify the correct statement regarding Mathura School of Art. a. Mathura School of Art is sometimes referred as the 'Kushan School of Art'. b. One of the chief characteristics of the Mathura figures is the elaborate and natural display of physical features like muscles, moustaches, etc c. The most striking example of the Mathura School of Art is the headless statue of Kanishka.
questions.explanation: The Mathura School of Art, which is sometimes referred as the 'Kushan School of Art', flourished at the holy city of Mathura, during 1-3 A.D. It existed side by side with the Gandhara Art under the Kushan rule and exerted a positive influence over it. The most striking example of the Mathura School of Art is the headless statue of Kanishka. Unlike the Gandhara sculptures, the Mathura figures do not have moustaches and beards. Hence statement b is incorrect. The chief characteristics of the Gandhara sculpture were: (i) depiction of life-like statues of Buddha in standing or seated positions; (ii) use of rich carving, elaborate ornamentation and complex symbolism, (iii) elaborate and natural display of physical features like muscles, moustaches, etc; (iv) portrayal of folds and turns of dresses and (v) great significance attached to refinement and polishing. Further insights: An important innovation of the Mathura School was the creation of an iconography, necessitated by the portrayal of various Bodhisattvas and Buddha images. The Mathura Buddhist images are characterised by prominent breasts and the form of drapery, which hangs down in semi-circular folds and covers both the shoulders. The 'mudras' or gestures of the Buddha image are also significant in the Mathura School. In the initial phases of this art the 'Abhinaya Mudra' was popular, which depicted a strongly-built Buddha with the right hand raised with open palm to signify benediction. The earliest sculptures of standing Buddha were made keeping the 'yaksha' prototype in mind and are indicative of the Kushan influence. Later sculptures feature the 'Dhyana Mudra', in which the Buddha in depicted in a sitting 'padmasana' posture. The Mathura School produced beautiful figures of the Buddha, the Jain Tirthankaras and deities of the Hindu pantheon made out of spotted red stone. This school of art flourished and got perfected under the Gupta rule.

questions.question: Which of the following statements is correct?
options: The five-year plan focuses mostly on private intervention in the economy.
The five-year plan always focused on the growth of the tertiary sector.
The five-year plan permits long-term intervention by the government in the economy.
A five-year plan always stresses market-oriented activities.
questions.explanation: The correct statement is the five-year plan permits long-term intervention by the government in the economy. The very essence of India's Five-Year Plans, adopted from the Soviet model, was to have a system of planned economic development. This involved the government (through the Planning Commission) setting long-term objectives for economic and social growth and actively intervening in the economy through resource allocation, public sector investments, and policy-making to achieve those goals over a five-year period. Explanation of Other Options: (a) is incorrect: For most of the planning era (especially before 1991), the focus was heavily on the public sector as the primary engine of growth, not private intervention. The private sector was often regulated and controlled. (b) is incorrect: The focus of the plans varied. The First Five-Year Plan focused on the primary sector (agriculture), and the Second focused on the secondary sector (heavy industries). The word "always" makes this statement false. (d) is incorrect: The Five-Year Plans were a tool of centralized planning, which is the opposite of a market-oriented approach where economic decisions are primarily driven by supply and demand. The shift towards market-oriented activities only began after the economic reforms of 1991. Further insights: India's Five-Year Plans were formulated and monitored by the Planning Commission from 1951 to 2014. In 2015, the Planning Commission was replaced by the NITI Aayog, marking a shift from centralized five-year planning to a more advisory and "think tank" role for the government's strategic planning.

questions.question: In November 2025, where did ISRO conduct the Integrated Main Parachute Airdrop Test (IMAT) for the Gaganyaan mission?
options: Babina Field Firing Range, Uttar Pradesh
questions.explanation: ISRO conducted the Integrated Main Parachute Airdrop Test (IMAT) for the Gaganyaan mission at the Babina Field Firing Range in Uttar Pradesh. The test was carried out to validate the parachute system that will safely bring the crew module back to Earth during the Gaganyaan mission. The wide and secure testing environment at Babina made it suitable for such aerial drop trials. Further Insights Gaganyaan is India's first human spaceflight mission. The mission aims to send Indian astronauts to Low Earth Orbit. The parachute system is crucial for safe splashdown recovery. The crew module will land in the sea after re-entry. ISRO has been conducting multiple abort and recovery tests before the final mission.

questions.question: Which term best describes the physical arrangement and location of the Kuiper Belt in relation to the Sun?
questions.explanation: · The Kuiper Belt is a circumstellar structure because it encircles the Sun. · "Circumstellar" means surrounding or orbiting a star. · The Kuiper Belt is a ring-like region beyond Neptune (30 - 55 AU), containing icy bodies and dwarf planets like Pluto, Makemake, and Haumea. Further Insights · The Kuiper Belt is the source of short-period comets (e.g., Halley-type). · Pluto, Eris, and Makemake are major Kuiper Belt Objects (KBOs). · The belt resembles a flattened disk of icy bodies. · It is believed to be a remnant of the primordial protoplanetary disk

questions.question: Which of the following is/are correct with respect to 'Census 2011'? I.In terms of Sex ratio among all states and Union territories Puducherry ranks second. II.In terms of Sex Ratio among the States and Union territories Daman and Diu ranks last. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanation: Here both statements are correct. Census 2011 data on Sex Ratio: Among all states Kerala has the highest Sex ratio. Then comes Puducherry. So, statement I is correct. Among all states and Union territories Daman and Diu has the lowest sex Ratio. So, statement II is correct too. Among all states Haryana has the lowest sex Ratio. Then comes Himachal Pradesh (2nd), then Sikkim and then Punjab (4th) from below. Source: India Year Book

questions.question: Identify the incorrect relationship(s) from the list below: (I) √17 + √7 = √11 + √13 (II) √17 + √7 < √11 + √13 (III) √17 + √7 > √11 + √13
On Simplification, the value of 712 - 812_________1012 - 912 is:
questions.explanation: 7 < 8 => 712 < 812 and 10 > 9 => 1012 > 912 (712 - 812) is a negative number and (1012 - 912) is a positive number. So, (712 - 812)/(1012 - 912) is a negative rational number.

questions.question: A chocolate factory is designing a special chocolate box shaped like a right triangular prism. The triangular base is a right-angled triangle with perpendicular sides measuring 5 cm and 12 cm. The height (thickness) of the box is 10 cm. What is the ratio of the area of the largest rectangular face to the area of the smallest rectangular face of the chocolate box?
questions.explanation: Hypotenuse = √(52 + 122) = 13 So, the sides of the triangle = 5 cm, 12 cm, and 13 cm The height of the prism = 10 cm Each rectangular face area = side of the triangle X height of prism Rectangle corresponding to side 5 cm = 5 * 10 = 50 cm2 Rectangle corresponding to side 12 cm = 12 * 10 = 120 cm2 Rectangle corresponding to hypotenuse 13 cm = 13 * 10 = 130 cm2 Hence, the required ratio = 130/50 = 13/5

questions.question: If the word PHOTOGRAPH is spelt with F' in place of 'PH', then what would be the percentage reduction in the number of letters?
questions.explanation: Number of letters in PHOTOGRAPH = 10 Number of letters in FOTOGRAF = 8 So, % reduction = (10 - 8)/10 * 100 = 20%

questions.question: Find the value of: 3 Sin A - 6 Sin3 A_________6 Cos3 A - 3 Cos A + Cot A
questions.explanation: ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p25359/image005.png) ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p25359/image006.png) ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p25359/image007.png) ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p25359/image008.png) ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p25359/image009.png) ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p25359/image010.png) ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p25359/image011.png)

questions.question: A shopkeeper sells an article at 10% loss to a person. Then this person marks the price of article at 20% above the cost price for him and allows a discount of 10% on marked price. What is the profit percentage of the person on selling the article?
questions.explanation: Let, the original cost price of the article = 100 units Cost price of article for the person = 100 * 90/100 = 90 units Marked price of the article = 90 * 120/100 = 108 units Selling price of the article = 108 * 90/100 = 97.2 units Required profit percentage = (97.2 - 90)/90 * 100 = 8 %

questions.question: In Δ ABC, AB = 7 cm, BC = 13 cm, and CA = X cm. Which of the following option is correct for the value of X?
questions.explanation: We know that third side of the triangle is always greater than the difference between the other two sides and less than the sum of length of other two sides. Therefore, X will always greater than 13 - 7 = 6 cm And X will always less than 13 + 7 = 20 cm So, 6 < X < 20.

questions.question: An event management company printed 1,200 VIP passes for a concert at a total cost of ₹24,00,000. They gave 100 VIP passes free to special guests. For the remaining passes, they offered a 20% discount on the marked price of ₹2,500 per pass. Additionally, they offered a scheme of Buy 4 VIP passes, Get 1 Free. If all 1,200 VIP passes were distributed, what is the overall gain or loss percentage for the company?
Two equal circles of radius 36 cm intersect each other, such that each passes through the centre of the other. The length of the common chord is:
questions.explanation: ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p29957/image013.png) AD = DB O1O2 = 36 cm O1A = O2A = 36 cm (Radius) ADO1 = 90˚ O1D = O2D = 18 cm AD = √(362 - 182) = 18√3 cm AB = 2 * 18√3 = 36√3 cm

questions.question: In what ratio does x-axis divide the line segment (6, -2) and (5, 7)?
questions.explanation: (x1, y1) and (x2, y2) are (6, -2) and (5, 7) respectively. Let the ratio = k:1 The coordinate, where x-axis cuts the line segment be (m, 0). Now, y = (ky2 + y1)/(k + 1) 0 = (7k - 2)/(k + 1) 7k - 2 = 0 k = 2/7 Hence the ratio is 2:7.

questions.question: A, B, and C invest in the same scheme. A and B invest the same amount. A withdraws 20% and B withdraws 15% of their investments. The difference between their withdrawals is Rs.300, and the sum of the withdrawals of A and B together is Rs.600 more than C's withdrawal.If C's withdrawal is 30% of C's investment then what was C's original investment?
questions.explanation: Let A's and B's investments = 100x each. A's withdrawal = 20x; B's = 15x; difference = 5x = 300 → x = 60 → A or B invests 6000. Sum withdrawals A+B = (20% + 15%) of 6000 = 35% of 6000 = 2100. C's withdrawal = 2100 − 600 = 1500 = 30% of C's investment C's investment = 1500 / 0.30 = Rs.5000.

questions.question: The volume of a cuboid is 1320 cm3. If its length is 20 cm and the breadth is 5 cm less than the height, then find the breadth of the cuboid.
questions.explanation: Let breadth of the cuboid = b cm Height of the cuboid = 'b + 5' cm Length = 20 cm Volume of a cuboid = 1320 cm3 20 * b * (b + 5) = 1320 b2 + 5b - 66 = 0 b2 + 11b - 6b - 66 = 0 (b + 11)(b - 6) = 0 b = -11, 6 So, breadth of the cuboid = 6 cm

questions.question: In the given figure, AB ∥ CD ∥ EF and the measure of ∠GJH and ∠GJC is 68° and 32°. Find the measure of ∠HIF. ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p29077/image002.png)
questions.explanation: ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p29077/image003.png) ∠GJH = 68°, ∠GJC = 32° Since, CD is a line => ∠GJH + ∠GJC + ∠HJD = 180° ∠HJD = 180 - 68 - 32 = 80° Since, ∠HJD and ∠HIF are corresponding angles, then they are equal. ∠HIF = ∠HJD = 80°

questions.question: A hollow sphere of outer diameter 10cm is cut into two equal hemispheres. The total surface area of one of the hemispheres is 286cm2. Each one of the hemispheres is filled with water. What is the volume (in cm3) of water that can be filled in each hemisphere?
questions.explanation: Suppose inner radius is r 2π[r2+52] + π[52-r2] = 286 π[2r2+50+25-r2] = 286 r2 + 75 = 286 X (7/22) r2 = 16 r = 4 Therefore, volume in each hemisphere is: (2/3) πr3 = 128&#960/3 cm 3

questions.question: A vertical cylindrical juice glass is filled with juice. A solid hemispherical ice cube of radius 12 cm is completely immersed in the juice, and the juice level rises by 5.12 cm. What is the radius of the cylindrical glass?
questions.explanation: Volume of hemisphere = 2/3 * πr3 Volume of cylinder = πR2H Volume of hemisphere = Volume of cylinder 2/3 * 22/7 * 123 = 22/7 * R2 * 5.12 R2 = 225 R = 15 cm

questions.question: A mixture of 72 litres contains milk and water in the ratio of 1:3, respectively. If 8 litres of the mixture is taken out and replaced with 18 litres of milk, then what is the ratio of milk and water in the final mixture, respectively?
questions.explanation: Quantity of mixture when 8 litres of mixture is taken out = 72 - 8 = 64 litres Quantity of milk in 64 litres of mixture = 64*1/4 = 16 litres Quantity of water in 64 litres of mixture = 64*3/4 = 48 litres The ratio of milk and water in the final mixture = (16 + 18): 48 = 34:48 = 17:24

questions.question: A principal of ₹10,000 is invested at 8% p.a. compound interest, compounded half-yearly for 1 year. What compound interest does it earn?
In a circle, a chord is drawn, which is equal to the radius of the circle. If the area of the minor segment formed is 27(2π - 3√3) cm2, then find the perimeter of the circle.
questions.explanation: Area of the minor segment formed = Area of the minor sector formed - area of equilateral triangle 27(2π - 3√3) = 60/360 * πr2 - √3r2/4 27(2π - 3√3) = (π/6 - √3/4) * r2 r2 = 27(2π - 3√3)/[(2π - 3√3)/12] r2 = 27*12 = 9*36 r = 18 Perimeter of the circle = 2*π*18 = 36π cm

questions.question: Three numbers are given in the form (x − 1) : (x + 2) : (2x + 1). If these three numbers are in arithmetic progression, what is their ratio?
questions.explanation: Let the three numbers be A = (x − 1)k, B = (x + 2)k, C = (2x + 1)k. For an arithmetic progression, B − A = C − B => (x + 2)k − (x − 1)k = (2x + 1)k − (x + 2)k => k(3) = k(x − 1) => 3 = x − 1 => x = 4 Thus the numbers are (4 − 1)k = 3k, (4 + 2)k = 6k, (2 X 4 + 1)k = 9k => Ratio 3 : 6 : 9

questions.question: A bag of salt of 200 gm contains 3.2 * 106 crystals. Find the quantity of salt that contains 8 * 104 crystals.
questions.explanation: 200 gm salt → 3.2 * 106 crystals Quantity of salt that contains 8 * 104 crystals = (200 * 8 * 104)/(3.2 * 106) = 5 gm

questions.question: M and N can complete 1/3rd of a work in 10 days and 2/5th of the work in 20 days respectively. If they work together, then in how many days the work will be completed?
questions.explanation: Ratio of time taken by M and N = (3*10):(5/2*20) = 3:5 Ratio of efficiency of M and N = 5:3 Time taken by M and N together to complete the work = (5/2*20)*3/(3 + 5) = 75/4 days

questions.question: A cliff is 255 m high above sea level. From the top of the cliff, the angles of depression of two fishermen standing on opposite sides of the cliff are 45° and 30°. Determine the distance between the two fishermen.
questions.explanation: ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p30781/image004.png) NM = Height of cliff CM = Distance between the base of the cliff and 1st fisherman DM = Distance between the base of the cliff and 2nd fisherman Tan 45˚ = NM/CM 1 = 255/CM CM = 255 m Tan 30˚ = NM/MD 1/√3 = 255/MD MD = 255√3 m Hence, the required distance = 255 (√3 + 1) m

questions.question: The average weight of 108 persons in an organization is 57.5 kg where (4/9)th of the total number of persons are women and the rest are men. The ratio of the average weight of men to that of the women is 41:35 respectively. The average weight (in kg) of the men is:
questions.explanation: (4/9)th of the total no. of persons are women, i.e., (4/9) * 108 = 48 women are there Hence, no. of men = 60 Let the average weights (in kg) of men and women be 41w and 35w respectively. Total weight of all the persons = 108 * 57.5 = 6210 Total weight of all the men = 60 * 41w = 2460w And total weight of all the women = 48 * 35w = 1680w Hence, 2460w + 1680w = 6210 w = 6210/4140 = 1.5 Hence, average weight (in kg) of the men = 41 * 1.5 = 61.5 kg

questions.question: A ΔPQR is right angled at Q and S is any point of the side PR such that QS ⊥ PR. If QS = 8 cm and PS2 + RS2 = 160 cm2, then find the length of PR.
questions.explanation: ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p16572/image001.png) PS2 + RS2 = 160 PQ2 - QS2 + QR2 - QS2 = 160 PQ2 + QR2 = 160 + 2QS2 PR2 = 160 + 128 = 288 PR = 12√2 cm

questions.question: Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the given word. Abetted
questions.explanation: 'Abetted' means encouraged or supported. 'Unauthorised' means something that has not been officially approved or permitted. So, the two words can be used as antonyms of each other. Hence, (c) is the correct answer. Endorsed- approved Encouraged- give support, confidence, or hope to (someone). Permissible- something that is allowed, acceptable, or permitted according to rules, laws, regulations, or standards.

questions.question: Spot the correct spelling for a sentence that changes its grammatical structure midway through.
questions.explanation: An anacoluthon is an unexpected discontinuity in the expression of ideas within a sentence, leading to a form of words in which there is logical or grammatical incoherence of thought. Hence, option (a) is the correct answer.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate meaning of the given idiom. Laugh all the way to the bank
options: The last in a series of unpleasant events that finally makes you feel that you cannot continue to accept a bad situation
Make a great deal of money very easily
An apparent misfortune that eventually has good results
questions.explanation: The idiom 'Laugh all the way to the bank' means 'Make a great deal of money very easily'. Hence option b) is the correct answer. 'The last or final straw ' means 'The last in a series of unpleasant events that finally makes you feel that you cannot continue to accept a bad situation'. 'Marry money' means 'Marry a rich person'. 'A blessing in disguise' means 'An apparent misfortune that eventually has good results'.

questions.question: Choose the correct semantic fit: The memo used deliberately ____ language, so employees interpreted it in completely different ways.
questions.explanation: Option A is correct because "ambiguous language" means unclear language that can be understood in more than one way, which matches "interpreted it in different ways." Meanings of all options: Ambiguous = unclear, having multiple meanings Ambitious = strongly determined to succeed Audible = able to be heard Arbitrary = based on random choice, not a clear reason · Option B is wrong because ambition is a quality of a person, not language clarity. · Option C is wrong because "audible" relates to sound, not meaning. · Option D is wrong because "arbitrary language" is not the right match for multiple interpretations. Final correct sentence: The memo used deliberately ambiguous language, so employees interpreted it in completely different ways.

questions.question: Choose the correct semantic fit: Her calm message turned out to be a ____ that restarted the stalled talks.
questions.explanation: The sentence needs a word meaning "something that triggers a positive change or restart." Option D: catalyst means something that causes or speeds up an event or change. Meanings of all options: A. catalogue = a list of items (like books/products) B. catapult = a device to throw something / to launch suddenly C. cataract = an eye condition OR a large waterfall D. catalyst = something that makes change happen faster So Option D matches the context best. · Option A is wrong because a "list" cannot restart talks. · Option B is wrong because it refers to launching/throwing, not "causing talks to restart" in meaning. · Option C is wrong because it does not fit the idea of negotiations restarting. Final sentence: Her calm message turned out to be a catalyst that restarted the stalled talks.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate option to fill in the blanks. Weather refers to the short-term atmospheric conditions in a specific place at a given time, such as temperature, humidity, and precipitation. Climate, on the other hand, _____ the long-term average of weather patterns over an extended period. While weather provides a snapshot of current conditions, climate offers a broader ________ on typical weather trends and patterns over time.
questions.explanation: The most appropriate option to fill in the first blank is 'describes'. 'Describes' means to give an account or representation of something. In the sentence, it indicates that climate represents or gives information about the long-term average of weather patterns. Other words do not fit the context of the sentence. 'Prepares' means to make ready. 'Alerts' means to warn. 'Summons' means to call for or demand. The most appropriate option to fill in the second blank is 'perspective'. 'Perspective' means a particular attitude toward or way of regarding something. In the sentence, it refers to the broader view or understanding that climate provides about typical weather trends and patterns over time. Other words do not fit the context of the sentence. 'Dwelling' means a place where people live. 'Domicile' also means a place of residence. 'Settlement' means a community or agreement. Hence, (b) is the correct answer.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate option to fill in the blank. The witness was clearly lying under oath, as her statements were full of contradictions and inconsistencies. She was committing ______.
questions.explanation: Perjury - the offence of wilfully telling an untruth in a court after having taken an oath or affirmation. (Correct - describes lying under oath) Slander - the action or crime of making a false spoken statement damaging to a person's reputation. (Spoken defamation, not necessarily under oath) Libel - a published false statement that is damaging to a person's reputation; a written defamation. (Written defamation) Blasphemy - the act or offence of speaking sacrilegiously about God or sacred things; profane talk. (Irreverence towards religious figures/concepts) Correct Sentence: The witness was clearly lying under oath, as her statements were full of contradictions and inconsistencies. She was committing perjury. Hence, option (a) is the answer.

questions.question: Select the option that can be used as a one-word substitute for the given group of words. Partly melted or watery snow
questions.explanation: 'Slush' is the appropriate one-word substitute for the given group of words. The term 'slush' describes snow that has partly melted or become watery, often forming a soft, wet, and sloppy mixture. 'Mutiny' refers to a rebellion or revolt against authority, especially by sailors or soldiers. 'Coffle' refers to a chain or rope used to restrain or connect prisoners. 'Plaintiff' refers to a person who brings a lawsuit or complaint against someone else. Hence, (a) is the correct answer.

questions.question: According to the passage, what is the primary goal of sustainable development?
options: Balancing economic growth with ecological preservation
questions.explanation: As per the following sentence from the first paragraph, "It seeks to balance economic growth with ecological preservation, ensuring that future generations inherit a viable planet," it can be noted that the primary goal of sustainable development is to balance economic growth and ecological preservation. Hence, the correct answer is option (d).

questions.question: What type of clause is used in the sentence: "Developing nations often face the difficult dilemma of pursuing economic progress while lacking the financial and technological resources to adopt cleaner alternatives rapidly."
questions.explanation: The correct answer is B. Adverbial clause because the clause "while lacking the financial and technological resources to adopt cleaner alternatives rapidly" modifies the verb phrase "face the difficult dilemma" and explains the condition under which developing nations experience this dilemma. The word "while" acts as a subordinating conjunction, introducing a dependent clause that provides information about circumstance or contrast. An adverbial clause typically answers questions such as when, why, how, or under what condition something happens. In this case, it answers the question, "Under what condition do developing nations face this dilemma?" Therefore, since the clause functions to modify the main verb by describing the situation or condition, it is an adverbial clause and not a noun clause, relative clause, or independent clause.

questions.question: What role does education play in sustainable development according to the passage?
options: It fosters awareness and empowers individuals.
questions.explanation: As per the following sentence from the last paragraph, "Education plays a pivotal role in fostering awareness, empowering individuals to make eco-friendly choices in daily life," it can be noted that education fosters positive contribution in the form of empowering individuals with eco-friendly choices. Hence, the correct answer is option (a).

questions.question: Which of the following assumptions does the author most strongly challenge in the passage?
options: Economic growth and environmental protection are always mutually exclusive goals.
Technological innovation alone can ensure the success of sustainable development.
Developing nations are unwilling to participate in global environmental initiatives.
Renewable energy sources are more expensive than fossil fuels in all contexts.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option (b) because the author clearly argues that although technology offers innovative solutions such as solar power, electric vehicles, and carbon capture systems, these measures alone are insufficient to achieve true sustainability. The passage emphasizes that long-term success depends on human behavior, cultural shifts, policy commitment, and international cooperation. By stating that even the most advanced technologies may fall short without collective commitment, the author challenges the assumption that technological innovation by itself can solve environmental problems. The other options either misrepresent the authors argument or introduce ideas not directly supported by the passage. Hence, option (b) is correct.

questions.question: What will ultimately determine the success of sustainable development efforts, as per the passage?
questions.explanation: As per the following sentence from the last paragraph, "Ultimately, while technology offers innovative solutions like solar power and electric vehicles, it is human behaviour and international cooperation that will determine the success of sustainable development efforts," it can be noted that human behaviour and international cooperation seem to be the decisive factors. Hence, the correct answer is option (c).

questions.question: The figure of speech used in "The newsroom applauded when the White House responded." is:
questions.explanation: C. Metonymy is correct because "the White House" is used to mean the U.S. administration/government, which is closely associated with that place. Metonymy means replacing the real thing with something closely related to it. Example: "The pen is mightier than the sword." (pen = writing, sword = force) · A. Synecdoche uses a part to represent the whole (like "wheels" for a car); here it is not part-for-whole, it is place-for-institution. · B. Hyperbole is exaggeration; not used here. · D. Onomatopoeia uses sound-words; not used here.

questions.question: Choose the appropriate antonym of the word: Mangle
questions.explanation: Mangle - destroy or severely damage by tearing or crushing. Maintain - cause or enable (a condition or situation) to continue. So, both words are opposite in meaning to each other. Hence, option (a) is the answer. Impose - force (an unwelcome decision or ruling) on someone.

questions.question: Select the correct Synonym of "EFFULGENT".
questions.explanation: "Effulgent" means shining brilliantly. (a) Somber = dark (b) Murky = cloudy or dim (c) Luminous = bright (d) Tenebrous = shadowy Only (c) shares brightness. Hence, option (c) is the answer.

questions.question: In the question given below, a sentence is given, divided into parts. One of the parts may contain an error. Identify the part that contains the error, and mark it as the answer. Ignore errors of punctuation. Because overt aggression is clearly a destructive force, some recent experiments suggest that anger, (A) / and related emotions like frustration or irritation, (B) / can also bring some advantages - provided we know (C) / how to channel the energy arising from those feelings. (D)
questions.explanation: The first part of the sentence has an error. 'Because' is incorrect in the context of this sentence, which provides two contrasting facts about anger. A word that expresses contrast like 'although' or 'while' is more suitable in case of this sentence. Hence, c) is the correct answer which indicates part A has an error.

questions.question: Identify the segment in the sentence which contains a grammatical error. The draft will be finalized once our boss will sign it.
questions.explanation: The error lies in option (d). Adverbial clauses of time consist of two clauses: the main clause and the time clause. The time clause begins with 'once' in this case. If the main clause refers to the future (will be), the simple present tense is used in the time clause. For example: Peter will telephone you once he returns. Similarly, in this question, the time clause begins with 'once' and the main clause refers to the future ('will be' implies it). So, 'signs' (Simple present) must replace 'will sign' in the sentence. Thus, the error is in option (d) will sign. Therefore, the corrected form of the sentence is: The draft will be finalized once our boss signs it.

questions.question: Select the option that will improve the underlined part of the given sentence. In case no improvement is needed, select 'No improvement required'. But if you want to save your cabbages from eating by caterpillars, you must use this solution.
questions.explanation: The sentence should be in passive voice because the action is done on the subject 'cabbages'. Therefore, the underlined part of the sentence is grammatically incorrect as it is in active voice. Option (a) is eliminated as it is also in active voice. Option (c) is eliminated because it is grammatically incorrect. We cannot use a present participle verb after 'being'. Option (b) is in passive voice [being + v3] and it fits correctly in the given sentence. Hence, option (b) is the correct answer. Note: Active and Passive Voice: When the person doing the action is the subject, the sentence is said to be in active form/voice. When the subject is what the action is directed at, then the sentence is said to be in passive form/voice. For example: Active Voice: Rita and her friends performed a skit. Here 'Rita and her friends' (subject) perform the action. Passive Voice: A skit was performed by Rita and her friends. Here 'skit' is the subject (at which the action is directed).

questions.question: Select the most appropriate meaning of the underlined idiom in the given sentence. The construction of a new factory at Avenue Street is on the carpet and it will be quite beneficial for the local people as it will generate large scale employment.
questions.explanation: On the carpet: to be under consideration. It is in context to the fact that the construction of a new factory at the Avenue Street is under consideration. The rest of the meanings are contextually incorrect. Therefore, the correct answer is:(b) Under consideration.

questions.question: In the following question, the first and the last parts of the sentence/passage are numbered 1 and 6. The rest of the sentence/passage is split into four parts - P, Q, R, and S. These four parts are not given in their proper order. Read the sentence/passage and find the most logical order of the parts between 1 and 6. 1: Renewable energy has emerged as a key solution to the growing global energy crisis. P: Among these, solar and wind power are the most rapidly expanding sources due to technological advancements and declining costs. Q: Unlike fossil fuels, renewables offer a sustainable and cleaner alternative that reduces greenhouse gas emissions. R: Governments worldwide are investing heavily in renewable infrastructure and incentivizing green innovation. S: However, challenges such as energy storage and grid integration continue to limit large-scale adoption. 6: The future of energy lies in balancing innovation, investment, and environmental responsibility.
questions.explanation: Opening Statement (1) Introduces renewable energy as a solution. Sentence (Q) Explains how it differs from fossil fuels. Sentence (P) Adds examples of major renewable sources. Sentence (R) Describes global efforts to promote renewables. Sentence (S) Addresses remaining technological challenges. Closing Statement (6) Concludes with the need for balanced progress. Thus, the logical order is QPRS. Hence, option (c) is correct.

questions.question: Sentences of a paragraph are given below in jumbled order. Arrange the sentences in the correct order to form a meaningful and coherent paragraph. A. In the 21st century, innovations in consumer technology have transformed the way individuals communicate, work, and engage with the world. B. From the ubiquitous presence of smartphones and the advent of smart homes to the rise of wearable devices and the integration of artificial intelligence in daily life, consumer technology has become deeply intertwined with societal progress. C. As consumers embrace the latest gadgets and applications, the ethical implications of data collection, algorithmic decision-making, and the potential for technological biases come under scrutiny. D. However, this innovation is not without its challenges, particularly concerning data privacy, security, and ethical considerations. E. The continuous pursuit of convenience, efficiency, and connectivity has driven the development of cutting-edge products and services.
questions.explanation: Sentence A serves as an introductory statement, setting the context by stating that in the 21st century, innovations in consumer technology have transformed the way individuals communicate, work, and engage with the world. Sentence B follows by providing examples of how consumer technology has evolved, mentioning the ubiquitous presence of smartphones, smart homes, wearable devices, and the integration of artificial intelligence in daily life. This sentence establishes the breadth of the impact of technology on societal progress. Sentence E continues by stating that the continuous pursuit of convenience, efficiency, and connectivity has driven the development of cutting-edge products and services. This reinforces the idea that technological advancements are motivated by a desire for improved functionality and interconnectedness. Sentence D introduces a note of caution by acknowledging that innovation in consumer technology is not without its challenges. It specifically mentions concerns related to data privacy, security, and ethical considerations. Finally, Sentence C concludes the paragraph by noting that as consumers embrace the latest gadgets and applications, ethical implications related to data collection, algorithmic decision-making, and potential technological biases come under scrutiny. This sentence brings attention to the ethical considerations associated with the rapid advancement of consumer technology. So, the correct order is ABEDC. Hence, (c) is the correct answer.

questions.question: Select the sentence that contains a spelling error.
options: After a long day at work, she enjoyed unwinding with a warm cup of tea.
The ancient castle stood majestically on the hill, telling stories of a glorious past and history.
He spent hours practising the piano, hoping to impress his family at the upcoming recital.
The children gathered around the campfire, sharing stories and roasting marshmellows under the starry sky.
questions.explanation: Sentence d) has incorrectly spelt the word - "marshmellows". The correct word is "marshmallows". It means "a soft, chewy item of confectionery made with sugar and gelatin." Hence, option (d) is the answer.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate meaning of the proverb underlined in this sentence. With tickets booked and bags packed, Kavya said the trip was as good as done but her father cautioned that there is many a slip between the cup and the lip.
options: Failing early is better than failing late
Even near certain plans can still go wrong unexpectedly
questions.explanation: The proverb "there is many a slip between the cup and the lip" means that even when success seems certain, unexpected problems can arise and prevent the outcome. In the sentence, although Kavya felt everything was ready for the trip, her father reminded her that things could still go wrong. So, the correct answer is: (d) Even near certain plans can still go wrong unexpectedly

questions.question: Select the correct Synonym of "SENTENTIOUS".
questions.explanation: "Sententious" means moralizing or using few words in a pompous way. (a) Terse = brief and to the point (b) Pithy = concise but forceful (c) Florid = ornate (d) Verbose = wordy Only (a) aligns with the conciseness aspect. Hence, option (a) is the answer.
